rulings.Case Title: JUAN CESPEDES et al VS. CENTERSTONE REALTY, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 25, 2010, line 1, DEFENDANT RONAN ERICKSON MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment is denied. Triable issue of fact raised whether defendant had the ability to disburse money from Centerstone's account and whether defendant misappropriated that money. See defendant's declaration pargraphs 8 - 11 and defendant's deposition pages 98-100. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JT VENTURES PARTNERS COCHIN FUND, L.P. et al VS. NETWORK GENERAL CENTRAL CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Network General Central Corporation And Michael Pope'S Motion For Summary Judgment (Transferred From Department 301)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 25, 2010, line 16, Defendants Network General Central Corporation and Michael Pope's Motion For Summary Judgment (Transferred From Department 301). Motion to summary judgment is denied. Motion for summary adjudication is denied. The Parole evidence rule is not a bar to these claims. Plaintiffs allege fraud in the inducement and the $3 per share allocations do not contradict the agreement, but merely adds a value to the stock. Economic loss rule does not apply because plaintiff's complaint includes causes of action based on tort, not contract. Triable issue of fact whether alleged misrepresentations are expressions of opinion or affirmations of fact. Pope had superior knowledge regarding the subject matter and represented the $3 price as a fact. As to the alleged misrepresentation that the strike price would be charged to $3, triable issue of fact raised whether Pope believed the price would be increased considering the other information Pope possessed, such as the internal evaluation conducted by Duff and Phelps. Triable issue of fact regarding plaintiffs' reliance. Although plaintiffs were sophisticated investors, Pope gave a reasonable explanation as to why the $3 price could not be included in the agreement (tax implications). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SHEILA SPARKS VS. U-HAUL CO. OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 25, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT U-HAUL CO. OF CALIFORNIA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant shifted the burden and no triable issue of material fact raised. The accident involving the truck occurred 6/11/07. (UMF 1). It is against defendnat's policy to permit visitors, clients, employees, or any other individuals to use rental vehicles without first executing either a rental agreement or an internal use agreement. (UMF 19) whenever a vehicle is leased or removed from the facility by an employee or customer, an agreement is enerated. (UMF 19-20) At the relevant time, the truck involved in the collision was not leased to a customer or taken off the premises under an internal or any other use agreement. (UMF 23-24). The vehicle was not authorized to be removed from the premises until 6/16/07. (UMF 22) The vehicle was taken in a manner contrary to policy, without permission. Plaintiff presents no evidence to support her assertion that the vehicle was taken with permission, but relies on speculation.=(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ANN GARRISON VS. ONE WEST BANK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 25, 2010, line 9, DEFENDANT GMAC MORTGAGE, LLC DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained as to all causes of action with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead specific allegations in each cause of action as to each defendant. The complaint must state how the attachments create liability as to each defendant. The second and fourth causes of action are uncertain as to which defendant(s) plaintiff alleges a contractual relationship. The third cause of action fails to plead fraud with specificity as to any defendant. The negligence cause of action also lacks specificity. No facts support breach of contract. =(302/CWW)
